prose:
Pyridine Fukui reactivity analysis was computed using DFT with B3LYP functional and def2-TZVP basis set (tier standard, density-fitted) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (neutral state; cation and anion also computed for descriptor derivation). Geometry: pyridine.xyz, not re-optimized. All states converged; Mulliken charge conservation checks passed. 

The most electrophilic site is the nitrogen atom (atom 1), with **f⁺ = 0.484** (Fukui electrophilicity descriptor), indicating strong affinity for nucleophilic attack. The most nucleophilic site is C-4 (meta carbon), with **f⁻ = 0.118** (Fukui nucleophilicity descriptor), most reactive toward electrophilic attack. Full per-atom Fukui descriptors (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰, dual descriptor) and Mulliken partial charges reported in the results table above. Visualization plot saved to the run directory. Integrity: trustworthy.
